kept.text:
Big news: @stripe payments data is now available on Databricks Marketplace. 

Activate the Stripe Data Pipeline in minutes, then query live payment data in Databricks to power models, agents and Genie workspaces
• Deploy an agent that monitors transactions continuously
• Combine transaction history with behavioral signals to score customers 
• Ask questions like "Show me MRR by geography" and get instant answers

No heavy ETL. Just governed, real-time access to the data that drives your business, and a feedback loop between payments and decision-making. https://t.co/E9OBL1QBwn

At #RSAC2026, Databricks co-founder and CEO @alighodsi showed how agents can take on security workflows that are manual today and often require hundreds of people.

With data in an open security lakehouse, agents can normalize and organize data, monitor and fix pipelines, write detections, enrich signals, and handle triage, investigation, and response across the lifecycle.

Watch the full keynote:
https://t.co/aYxTYfOxDt

profile_bio.description: Databricks is the Data and AI company, helping organizations build and scale data and AI apps, analytics and agents.

kept.text:
Tomorrow is the last day of the 15-week challenge that started January 6th.

97 students have been listening to me yap and building projects for months about:
- Analytics Engineering - Snowflake, Airflow (via Astronomer), Trino (via Starburst), Iceberg and dbt Labs
- Data Engineering - Databricks, Delta Table, Apache Spark, and AgentBricks
- AI Engineering - LangChain, OpenClaw, Claude Code, and Anthropic Managed agents.

We have covered an unbelievable amount of ground in this time!

A shoutout to a few students who have been so engaged the entire time

Nayana Jabashetti - she implemented some of the Delta table techniques immediately at her job to improve pipeline efficiency over 10x!

Lubo Bali - he landed an AI engineer job in the middle of the boot camp and has made https://t.co/czKfKRB2hr, a very promising SaaS product

Tiffani Anderson - she came in with basically no experience in data and AI and is leaving with tons of confidence in her abilities

I'll be giving some more shoutouts to other really amazing students who left an impact on me as an instructor!

I want to express my immense gratitude to students for their hard work and attention over these last 4 months!

kept.text:
𝗜 𝗮𝗹𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗹𝗲𝘁 𝗮 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗜 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗮 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗵 𝗮𝗴𝗼 𝗱𝗶𝗲 𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗲𝘁𝗹𝘆.
 
Today I came back.
 
One session. More progress than 4 weeks of stop-start.
 
Here’s what I found inside the “working” code:
 
A function called clean_products_silver() referenced in cell 24. Didn’t exist anywhere in the notebook.
 
Ghost function. From a dead session. Silently killing everything downstream.
 
No error. No warning. Just broken.
 
Then I found the SCD2 that wasn’t actually SCD2.
 
whenMatchedUpdateAll() Overwrites records in place. No history. No versioning.
 
Type 1 dressed up as Type 2.
 
Then Spark tried to embarrass me.
 
isin("-", "", None) looks fine. But Spark ignores None inside isin().
 
Nulls were slipping through undetected. Corrupting the is_current flag the whole way down.
 
Fix: isNull() explicitly. Always.
 
End of session:
 
→ crm_prd_info Silver — 397 records 197 current / 200 historical / 0 duplicates
 
→ crm_cust_info Silver — 18,484 records schema conflict resolved
 
→ crm_sales_details Silver — 60,398 records 0 nulls / 0 bad dates
 
SHA-256 surrogate keys. Proper SCD2 write logic. Incremental append for facts.
 
Going quiet isn’t failure.
 
𝗡𝗼𝘁 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗯𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝗶𝘀.

#DataFam #Dataengineering #Databricks

kept.text:
The most anticipated IPO pipeline in years is almost here.

Some of the most valuable companies ever built are about to become public.

~$4T in combined valuation.

The biggest IPO class most investors have ever seen:

SpaceX: $1.75T target valuation.
OpenAI: $1T target.
Anthropic: $380B valuation.
Stripe: $159B (Feb 2026 tender)
Databricks: $134B.
Revolut: Targeting $150B to $200B.
Canva: $42B to $56B.
Kraken: $20B.
Shein: filed confidentially.
ConsenSys (MetaMask): $7B.
Bolt: €6.3B.
MiniMax: Raised ~$538M (live)

The big three alone could add ~$3T (SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic).

kept.text:
Amazon, Microsoft, VISA, Databricks, Mastercard, Target, Walmart

These are some of top companies, where many of my students are working on real-world complex data engineering systems today.

I always believed that, cracking interview is just a part of process.

What’s most important is how good are your fundamentals and though process, so that you can be known as one of the best Data Engineers.

Listing one student from each of above companies.

Jiku Sandilya - Data Engineer 2 at Amazon
Chitransh Saxena - Data Engineer & AI Consultant at Microsoft
P Asritha Sai - Data Engineer at VISA
Himanshu Keshari - Senior Data Engineer at Databricks
Milon Das - Senior Data Engineer at Mastercard
Devesh Gupta - Senior Data Engineer at Target
Shilpi Srivastava - Data Engineer 3 at Walmart

Do remember, all these didn’t happen overnight.
There was a time in everyone’s career when they took a hard decision to transition and today, the outcome speaks for itself.

If you are planning to transition to Data Engineering or already working in this domain Data Engineering, ask yourself;

Are you preparing just to crack interviews,
or to become someone companies don’t want to lose?

tweet.text:
@conorsen Conor! I’ve dedicated the last 4 months of my life to this problem!

Use Databricks ai_parse_document and ai_extract! We’ve trained models optimized for converting PDFs into text, tables, and image descriptions and extracting entities from them. Doesn’t matter how many pages are in your PDF or if the pages are crinkled, the words are crossed out with handwriting in the margins.

We can process thousands of documents and extract hundreds of fields by batch processing with our spark runtime! Just add a Claude skill pointing to our docs :)

Do not use Claude Opus, you’re just wasting tokens and time! Feel free to dm me, would be happy to help you never experience this again 😉
